# Phase 3 STEPS - Prompt:

Analyze the attached Phase 3 Context Markdown content.


Extract and translate ALL daily steps, checklists, and agent tasks from the entire document.


## CRITICAL TIMELINE BOUNDARY CONSTRAINTS:
#### 1. STRICT PHASE DURATION LIMIT: Each individual Phase MUST be strictly bounded between 1 to 7 days maximum (Absolute Hard Limit: Maximum 7 days per phase). Under no circumstances are you allowed to invent, extrapolate, or generate scheduling logs beyond Day 7.
#### 2. PROGRESSION STOPPING CRITERION: Stop generating immediately once the core technical objectives of the current Phase are satisfied. Do NOT duplicate or loop previous task structures just to inflate the timeline. If the work is complete on Day 1, freeze the output and exit.

## AGENT ATOMICITY CONSTRAINTS (CRITICAL FOR PRODUCTION):
#### 1. ATOMIC AGENT ASSIGNMENT: Every single object inside the 'sub_tasks' array MUST have exactly ONE sub-agent role (string) assigned to the 'agent' field. Dual-agent or multi-agent assignments within a single task object are strictly forbidden.
#### 2. ONE AGENT PER WORK-UNIT: If a workflow step requires collaborative action (e.g., a Coder writes code and a Tester verifies it), you MUST split this action into two distinct, sequential task objects inside the 'sub_tasks' array:
   - Task 1: Assigned to "Coder" with its respective files in 'components'.
   - Task 2: Assigned to "Tester" with its respective verification files in 'components'.
#### 3. COMPONENT SEGREGATION: Ensure that the 'components' array inside each task object ONLY contains the specific files that the assigned agent will touch, create, or modify for that exact step.

## CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS FOR PRODUCTION STABILITY:


#### 1. Target Range Focus: Extract every single scheduled day found in the provided markdown file.
#### 2. Mandatory Data Extraction: You MUST parse and generate a day object node inside the 'days' array for EVERY single day documented in the text.

#### 3. NO ESCAPE HATCH: Do NOT return an empty array for 'days' under any circumstances if there is markdown text present. Even if tasks are not explicitly labeled, parse the paragraph descriptions into technical sub-tasks for that day.
#### 4. STRICT LITERAL FIELD VALUES (MANDATORY):
   - Populate the exact string "{global_context_file}" into the 'global_context_file' field.
   - Populate the exact string "sources/" into the 'source_target_dir' field.
#### 5. Task Details: For every micro task item under a specific day:
   - Provide a sequential task description text into the 'task' field.
   - Provide the assigned role (e.g., 'Coder', 'Tester', 'Reviewer', 'DevOps') into the 'agent', 'subAgent', 'assignee' or 'subAgent' field.
#### 6. Context Fields: For each day object, set 'day' as the integer value of that day, set 'context_file' to '{project_phase_context_file}', and set 'context_section' to 'DAY ' followed by the day number.

You MUST conform strictly to your required JSON Schema layout design structure:
{
  "$defs": {
    "DailyStep": {
      "properties": {
        "day": {
          "description": "Timeline iteration day inside this isolated phase.",
          "title": "Day",
          "type": "integer"
        },
        "context_file": {
          "description": "The phase context Markdown file for closure on this day.",
          "title": "Context File",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "context_section": {
          "description": "The day targeted for closure on this day.",
          "title": "Context Section",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "sub_tasks": {
          "description": "Array of isolated micro-tasks assigned to sub-agents.",
          "items": {
            "$ref": "##/$defs/SubAgentTask"
          },
          "title": "Sub Tasks",
          "type": "array"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "day",
        "context_file",
        "context_section",
        "sub_tasks"
      ],
      "title": "DailyStep",
      "type": "object"
    },
    "SubAgentTask": {
      "properties": {
        "id": {
          "description": "Sub-Task identity of Task that sub-agent role executing.",
          "title": "Id",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "agent": {
          "description": "Target sub-agent role executing the task.",
          "title": "Agent",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "desc": {
          "description": "Literal, low-level technical step assigned to the agent.",
          "title": "Desc",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "components": {
          "description": "Flat array of physical localized file paths or scripts modified or targeted by this single task. Return an empty array [] if no files are involved.",
          "items": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "title": "Components",
          "type": "array"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "id",
        "agent",
        "desc"
      ],
      "title": "SubAgentTask",
      "type": "object"
    }
  },
  "properties": {
    "phase_id": {
      "description": "Target phase tracker index.",
      "title": "Phase Id",
      "type": "integer"
    },
    "phase_name": {
      "description": "Target phase tracker name.",
      "title": "Phase Name",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project_name": {
      "description": "Target project tracker name.",
      "title": "Project Name",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "global_context_file": {
      "description": "Project global context Markdown file for closure.",
      "title": "Global Context File",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "source_target_dir": {
      "description": "Project sources folder path for closure.",
      "title": "Source Target Dir",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "days": {
      "description": "Day-by-day engineering tracking steps.",
      "items": {
        "$ref": "##/$defs/DailyStep"
      },
      "title": "Days",
      "type": "array"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "phase_id",
    "phase_name",
    "project_name",
    "global_context_file",
    "source_target_dir",
    "days"
  ],
  "title": "PhaseStepsPlan",
  "type": "object"
}

--- PHASE 3 CONTEXT MARKDOWN ---
## PHASE 3 CONTEXT BLUEPRINT: test-ai-architecture
#### 1. Phase Operational Scope & Objectives
| ## | Objective | Description |
|---|-----------|-------------|
| **3.1** | **Create a multiвЂ‘lingual, SEOвЂ‘ready Next.js frontвЂ‘end** | Build a unified frontвЂ‘end that serves both the web portal and the mobile app (iOS/Android) using a single Next.js codebase. Implement serverвЂ‘side rendering (SSR) and static generation (SSG) with proper metaвЂ‘tags, sitemaps, and Open Graph for SEO in all supported languages. |
| **3.2** | **Integrate authentication flows** | вЂў Internal email/password signвЂ‘in / signвЂ‘up (secure password hashing, email verification).<br>вЂў External OAuth providers: Firebase Auth, Google, Facebook.<br>вЂў Centralized auth state management (e.g., React Context / Zustand) with protected routes. |
| **3.3** | **Implement i18n & locale detection** | вЂў Detect user locale: stored preference в†’ browser AcceptвЂ‘Language в†’ fallback to `en`.<br>вЂў Use Next.js builtвЂ‘in i18n (`next-i18next` or `i18next`) with namespaceвЂ‘based resource loading.<br>вЂў Provide language switcher UI and SEO hreflang tags. |
| **3.4** | **Expose attendance & student APIs** | Consume Quarkus REST endpoints (`/api/students/*`, `/api/attendance/*`, `/api/notifications/*`) via a centralized API client. Implement QRвЂ‘scan UI that calls the attendance endpoint and displays remaining validity days. |
| **3.5** | **Add realвЂ‘time notifications & messaging** | вЂў Push notifications to mobile devices (Firebase Cloud Messaging).<br>вЂў Send Zalo messages to the studentвЂ™s phone number and to the associated Zalo group.<br>вЂў UI toast / inвЂ‘app alerts for immediate feedback. |
| **3.6** | **Deliver CI/CD artifacts for GKE** | Generate Docker image (`frontend:latest`) with all static assets, serverвЂ‘side bundles, and i18n files. Produce a `Dockerfile` and `docker-compose.yml` (if needed) ready for the DevOps pipeline. |
| **3.7** | **Establish quality gates** | Write unit, integration, and E2E tests covering component rendering, i18n switching, auth flows, API mocking, and SEO metaвЂ‘generation. Ensure test coverage в‰ҐвЂЇ80вЂЇ% and linting/formatting compliance. |

#### 2. Allowed Technical Scope & Directory Boundaries (Files, paths, and endpoints)

```
frontend/
в”њв”Ђ src/
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ __types/
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ components/          ## Reusable UI atoms, molecules, organisms
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ pages/                ## Next.js pages (e.g., /login, /students, /attendance)
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ api/                  ## API client wrappers (axios/interceptors)
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ services/             ## Business logic (auth, attendance, notifications)
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ hooks/                ## Custom React hooks (useAuth, useQRScanner, etc.)
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ utils/                ## i18n helpers, date formatting, QR generation
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ stores/               ## Zustand / Recoil global state (auth, ui)
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ i18n/                 ## Localization resources (en/, vi/, etc.)
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ styles/               ## Global CSS, Tailwind config
в”‚  в””в”Ђ tests/                ## Jest / React Testing Library unit & integration tests
в”њв”Ђ public/
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ manifests/            ## Web App Manifest for PWA
в”‚  в””в”Ђ icons/                ## Favicons, splash screens for mobile
в”њв”Ђ .next/                   ## Next.js build output (ignored in repo)
в”њв”Ђ next.config.js
в”њв”Ђ package.json
в”њв”Ђ Dockerfile               ## Builds image for GKE
в””в”Ђ docker-compose.yml       ## Optional local dev orchestration
```

**Key API Endpoints (Quarkus side вЂ“ assumed)**
- `GET /api/students/{id}` вЂ“ retrieve student profile
- `POST /api/attendance/scan` вЂ“ QR attendance submission (body: `{studentId, timestamp}`)
- `GET /api/students/{id}/validity` вЂ“ returns remaining validity days
- `POST /api/notifications/push` вЂ“ send FCM notification
- `POST /api/notifications/zalo` вЂ“ send Zalo message (body: `{phone, groupId, message}`)

All frontend calls must be prefixed with the base path `/api/` (Next.js rewrites or environment variable `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL`).

#### 3. Dedicated Sub-Agent Functional Directives

###### 3.1 Coder (Frontend Development)
- **Task C1.1** вЂ“ Scaffold Next.js project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and `next-i18next`.
- **Task C1.2** вЂ“ Implement locale detection middleware (`i18nMiddleware`) that reads cookie `locale` в†’ `Accept-Language` в†’ default `en`.
- **Task C1.3** вЂ“ Build authentication module:
  - Internal signвЂ‘up / signвЂ‘in forms with validation (Zod).
  - OAuth buttons linking to Firebase, Google, Facebook (use `react-firebase-ui` / `next-auth` patterns).
  - Protected route guard (`_app.tsx`) that redirects unauthenticated users.
- **Task C1.4** вЂ“ Create reusable UI components: `LanguageSwitcher`, `ProtectedRoute`, `QRScanner` (using `html5-qrcode`), `ValidityBadge`, `NotificationToast`.
- **Task C1.5** вЂ“ Develop `pages/`:
  - `/login` вЂ“ auth entry point.
  - `/students` вЂ“ list of students (admin view).
  - `/students/[id]` вЂ“ student detail with validity badge.
  - `/attendance` вЂ“ QR scanner UI that POSTs to `/api/attendance/scan` and shows success/feedback.
  - `/notifications` вЂ“ view sent Zalo/FCM messages.
- **Task C1.6** вЂ“ Write API client (`api/client.ts`) with interceptors for auth token handling and error mapping.
- **Task C1.7** вЂ“ Integrate attendance flow:
  - Call `/api/attendance/scan` on successful QR read.
  - Call `/api/students/{id}/validity` to refresh badge.
  - Display realвЂ‘time feedback (toast) and update UI state.
- **Task C1.8** вЂ“ Implement notification dispatch:
  - UI form to select Zalo group/phone and message.
  - Trigger `POST /api/notifications/zalo` and `POST /api/notifications/push`.
  - Show confirmation and error states.
- **Task C1.9** вЂ“ Configure `next.config.js` for i18n (locales, defaultLocale, localeDetection) and SEO (`generateRobotsTxt`, `generateSitemap`).
- **Task C1.10** вЂ“ Create `Dockerfile` and `docker-compose.yml` for containerizing the Next.js app (multiвЂ‘stage build, copy `package.json`, `next build`, serve with `nginx` or `node`).

###### 3.2 Tester (Quality Assurance)
- **Task T1.1** вЂ“ Write unit tests for utility functions (`localeDetector`, `dateFormatter`) using Jest + tsвЂ‘jest.
- **Task T1.2** вЂ“ Create component unit tests (React Testing Library) for `LanguageSwitcher`, `ProtectedRoute`, `QRScanner`, `ValidityBadge`.
- **Task T1.3** вЂ“ Mock API endpoints (`msw`) and test integration flows:
  - Login with internal credentials.
  - OAuth redirect simulation.
  - QR scan в†’ attendance POST в†’ validity GET.
  - Notification send в†’ Zalo/FCM mock responses.
- **Task T1.4** вЂ“ Develop E2E tests (Cypress) covering:
  - MultiвЂ‘language navigation (EN в†’ VI).
  - Protected route redirection.
  - EndвЂ‘toвЂ‘end attendance scanning scenario.
  - Notification sending workflow.
- **Task T1.5** вЂ“ Run accessibility audits (axe) and SEO checks (crawler simulation) for each locale.
- **Task T1.6** вЂ“ Generate test coverage report; ensure в‰ҐвЂЇ80вЂЇ% coverage before handвЂ‘off.
- **Task T1.7** вЂ“ Document test results and any known issues in a shared test report.

###### 3.3 Reviewer (Code Review & Standards)
- **Task R1.1** вЂ“ Perform static code analysis (ESLint, Prettier, SonarQube) on all committed files.
- **Task R1.2** вЂ“ Review pull requests for:
  - Adherence to naming conventions (`kebab-case` for files, `camelCase` for vars).
  - Security best practices (no hardвЂ‘coded credentials, proper CORS handling).
  - i18n usage (no English literals in component files).
  - Component composition (DRY, reusable atoms).
- **Task R1.3** вЂ“ Validate Docker images for size and vulnerability scanning (Trivy).
- **Task R1.4** вЂ“ Approve final build artifacts and signвЂ‘off for DevOps handвЂ‘off.

###### 3.4 DevOps (CI/CD & Deployment Prep)
- **Task D1.1** вЂ“ Define GitHub Actions workflow:
  - Lint в†’ Test в†’ Build в†’ Docker Build в†’ Push to GCR.
- **Task D1.2** вЂ“ Create `k8s/` manifests (Deployment, Service, Ingress) referencing the built image.
- **Task D1.3** вЂ“ Set up monitoring hooks (Prometheus/Grafana) for frontend metrics (response time, error rate) and logging (ELK stack).
- **Task D1.4** вЂ“ Prepare secret management (GCP Secret Manager) for OAuth credentials and Firebase keys.
- **Task D1.5** вЂ“ Verify that the Docker image runs correctly in a local GKE cluster (kind) before final signвЂ‘off.

###### 3.5 Manager (Phase Oversight)
- **Task M1.1** вЂ“ Track progress against the 7вЂ‘day window (DaysвЂЇ8вЂ‘14) and raise blockers.
- **Task M1.2** вЂ“ Ensure all subвЂ‘agent deliverables are logged in the phaseвЂ‘specific artifact repository.
- **Task M1.3** вЂ“ Approve transition criteria before PhaseвЂЇ4 kickoff.

#### 4. Phase Definition of Done (DoD)

- **Functional Deliverables**
  - Fully functional Next.js frontвЂ‘end serving web and mobile UI.
  - MultiвЂ‘language support with locale detection, language switcher, and hreflang SEO tags.
  - Internal & external authentication flows integrated and protected routes enforced.
  - QRвЂ‘scan attendance module that calls the Quarkus attendance endpoint and displays remaining validity days.
  - Notification module capable of sending Zalo messages and FCM push notifications.
  - All API integrations (students, attendance, notifications) mocked/functional in development.

- **Technical Artifacts**
  - `frontend/` repository with all source code, i18n resources, component library, and tests.
  - `Dockerfile` and `docker-compose.yml` ready for GKE deployment.
  - `k8s/` manifests (Deployment, Service, Ingress) generated.
  - CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions) defined, tested, and able to build/push Docker image to GCR.
  - Monitoring & logging configuration (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK) linked to the frontend.

- **Quality Gates**
  - Unit test coverage в‰ҐвЂЇ80вЂЇ% (Jest) and E2E test suite passing.
  - Accessibility (WCAGвЂЇ2.1 AA) and SEO audits completed for each locale.
  - Code review checklist fully satisfied; no security findings.
  - Docker image scanned вЂ“ no critical vulnerabilities.
  - All subвЂ‘agent tasks documented and signed off.

- **Phase Closure**
  - All deliverables merged into the main branch, tagged with `v0.3.0-frontend`.
  - PhaseвЂ‘3 retrospective completed; lessons captured for PhaseвЂЇ4.
  - Ready for handвЂ‘off to **PhaseвЂЇ4: Testing and Quality Assurance**.

*End of PhaseвЂЇ3 Blueprint.*
------------------------------------------

# Phase 4 STEPS - Prompt:

Analyze the attached Phase 4 Context Markdown content.


Extract and translate ALL daily steps, checklists, and agent tasks from the entire document.


## CRITICAL TIMELINE BOUNDARY CONSTRAINTS:
#### 1. STRICT PHASE DURATION LIMIT: Each individual Phase MUST be strictly bounded between 1 to 7 days maximum (Absolute Hard Limit: Maximum 7 days per phase). Under no circumstances are you allowed to invent, extrapolate, or generate scheduling logs beyond Day 7.
#### 2. PROGRESSION STOPPING CRITERION: Stop generating immediately once the core technical objectives of the current Phase are satisfied. Do NOT duplicate or loop previous task structures just to inflate the timeline. If the work is complete on Day 1, freeze the output and exit.

## AGENT ATOMICITY CONSTRAINTS (CRITICAL FOR PRODUCTION):
#### 1. ATOMIC AGENT ASSIGNMENT: Every single object inside the 'sub_tasks' array MUST have exactly ONE sub-agent role (string) assigned to the 'agent' field. Dual-agent or multi-agent assignments within a single task object are strictly forbidden.
#### 2. ONE AGENT PER WORK-UNIT: If a workflow step requires collaborative action (e.g., a Coder writes code and a Tester verifies it), you MUST split this action into two distinct, sequential task objects inside the 'sub_tasks' array:
   - Task 1: Assigned to "Coder" with its respective files in 'components'.
   - Task 2: Assigned to "Tester" with its respective verification files in 'components'.
#### 3. COMPONENT SEGREGATION: Ensure that the 'components' array inside each task object ONLY contains the specific files that the assigned agent will touch, create, or modify for that exact step.

## CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS FOR PRODUCTION STABILITY:


#### 1. Target Range Focus: Extract every single scheduled day found in the provided markdown file.
#### 2. Mandatory Data Extraction: You MUST parse and generate a day object node inside the 'days' array for EVERY single day documented in the text.

#### 3. NO ESCAPE HATCH: Do NOT return an empty array for 'days' under any circumstances if there is markdown text present. Even if tasks are not explicitly labeled, parse the paragraph descriptions into technical sub-tasks for that day.
#### 4. STRICT LITERAL FIELD VALUES (MANDATORY):
   - Populate the exact string "{global_context_file}" into the 'global_context_file' field.
   - Populate the exact string "sources/" into the 'source_target_dir' field.
#### 5. Task Details: For every micro task item under a specific day:
   - Provide a sequential task description text into the 'task' field.
   - Provide the assigned role (e.g., 'Coder', 'Tester', 'Reviewer', 'DevOps') into the 'agent', 'subAgent', 'assignee' or 'subAgent' field.
#### 6. Context Fields: For each day object, set 'day' as the integer value of that day, set 'context_file' to '{project_phase_context_file}', and set 'context_section' to 'DAY ' followed by the day number.

You MUST conform strictly to your required JSON Schema layout design structure:
{
  "$defs": {
    "DailyStep": {
      "properties": {
        "day": {
          "description": "Timeline iteration day inside this isolated phase.",
          "title": "Day",
          "type": "integer"
        },
        "context_file": {
          "description": "The phase context Markdown file for closure on this day.",
          "title": "Context File",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "context_section": {
          "description": "The day targeted for closure on this day.",
          "title": "Context Section",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "sub_tasks": {
          "description": "Array of isolated micro-tasks assigned to sub-agents.",
          "items": {
            "$ref": "##/$defs/SubAgentTask"
          },
          "title": "Sub Tasks",
          "type": "array"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "day",
        "context_file",
        "context_section",
        "sub_tasks"
      ],
      "title": "DailyStep",
      "type": "object"
    },
    "SubAgentTask": {
      "properties": {
        "id": {
          "description": "Sub-Task identity of Task that sub-agent role executing.",
          "title": "Id",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "agent": {
          "description": "Target sub-agent role executing the task.",
          "title": "Agent",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "desc": {
          "description": "Literal, low-level technical step assigned to the agent.",
          "title": "Desc",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "components": {
          "description": "Flat array of physical localized file paths or scripts modified or targeted by this single task. Return an empty array [] if no files are involved.",
          "items": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "title": "Components",
          "type": "array"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "id",
        "agent",
        "desc"
      ],
      "title": "SubAgentTask",
      "type": "object"
    }
  },
  "properties": {
    "phase_id": {
      "description": "Target phase tracker index.",
      "title": "Phase Id",
      "type": "integer"
    },
    "phase_name": {
      "description": "Target phase tracker name.",
      "title": "Phase Name",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project_name": {
      "description": "Target project tracker name.",
      "title": "Project Name",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "global_context_file": {
      "description": "Project global context Markdown file for closure.",
      "title": "Global Context File",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "source_target_dir": {
      "description": "Project sources folder path for closure.",
      "title": "Source Target Dir",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "days": {
      "description": "Day-by-day engineering tracking steps.",
      "items": {
        "$ref": "##/$defs/DailyStep"
      },
      "title": "Days",
      "type": "array"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "phase_id",
    "phase_name",
    "project_name",
    "global_context_file",
    "source_target_dir",
    "days"
  ],
  "title": "PhaseStepsPlan",
  "type": "object"
}

--- PHASE 4 CONTEXT MARKDOWN ---
## PHASE 4 CONTEXT BLUEPRINT: test-ai-architecture
#### 1. Phase Operational Scope & Objectives
| Item | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Goal** | Validate that the membershipвЂ‘hub application meets functional, nonвЂ‘functional, security, and compliance requirements through a comprehensive testing regimen. |
| **Key Objectives** | 1. **Functional correctness** вЂ“ Verify attendance QR scanning, user authentication (email/password + Firebase/Google/Facebook), multiвЂ‘center data isolation, expirationвЂ‘day calculation, and notification delivery (SMS/Zalo/app). <br>2. **Performance & Scalability** вЂ“ Confirm that Quarkus services, Kafka streams, and Postgres queries sustain expected load and scale horizontally in GKE. <br>3. **Security & Compliance** вЂ“ Execute penetration, OWASP, GDPR/CCPA dataвЂ‘privacy, and tokenвЂ‘handling tests; enforce leastвЂ‘privilege access controls. <br>4. **Quality Assurance** вЂ“ Achieve в‰ҐвЂЇ90вЂЇ% unitвЂ‘test coverage, endвЂ‘toвЂ‘end UI test pass rate, and automated regression suite execution. <br>5. **Observability** вЂ“ Validate logging, metrics, and alerting pipelines (Cloud Logging, Cloud Monitoring) are correctly instrumented and produce actionable data. |
| **Time Box** | Maximum 7 calendar days (DayвЂЇ15вЂ‘21 of the overall project). Work stops as soon as the core technical objectives are satisfied. |
| **Deliverables** | вЂў Test suite (unit, integration, UI, security, performance). <br>вЂў TestвЂ‘environment artifacts (Docker Compose/K8s manifests for test clusters). <br>вЂў CI/CD testвЂ‘pipeline configuration (GitHub Actions / Cloud Build). <br>вЂў TestвЂ‘execution reports and defectвЂ‘tracking logs. <br>вЂў Monitoring & logging validation report. |

#### 2. Allowed Technical Scope & Directory Boundaries (Files, paths, and endpoints)
| Category | Path / Endpoint | Rationale |
|----------|----------------|-----------|
| **Backend Test Sources** | `src/test/java/` (Quarkus) <br> `src/test/resources/` (Kafka fixtures, DB scripts) | Unit & integration tests for core services (AuthService, AttendanceService, NotificationService, CenterService). |
| **Frontend Test Sources** | `frontend/tests/` (Next.js unit & integration) <br> `frontend/e2e/` (Cypress) | UI and API contract validation for web & PWA. |
| **Mobile Test Sources** | `mobile/tests/` (ReactвЂ‘Native/Expo unit) <br> `mobile/e2e/` (Detox) | MultiвЂ‘platform UI and offlineвЂ‘first behavior. |
| **Security Test Harness** | `security/` (OWASP ZAP scripts, tokenвЂ‘validation tests) | Automated security scanning. |
| **Performance Test Harness** | `performance/` (k6 scripts targeting `/api/attendance/*`, `/api/auth/*`, Kafka topics) | Load & stress validation. |
| **Compliance Test Harness** | `compliance/` (GDPR/CCPA dataвЂ‘export/import tests, consentвЂ‘management checks) | Regulatory validation. |
| **CI/CD Test Config** | `.github/workflows/test.yml` <br> `docker-compose.test.yml` <br> `k8s/test/` (GKE test namespace manifests) | Automated test execution in isolated environments. |
| **Logging & Metrics** | `src/main/resources/logback.xml` <br> `src/main/resources/application.yml` (metrics endpoints) | Ensure observability instrumentation. |
| **API Endpoints (tested)** | `POST /api/auth/login` <br> `POST /api/auth/register` <br> `POST /api/attendance/qr/scan` <br> `GET /api/attendance/{id}/validity` <br> `POST /api/notifications/send` <br> `GET /api/centers/{id}/students` | Core business functions. |
| **Kafka Topics (tested)** | `attendance-events` <br> `notification-events` | EventвЂ‘driven flows. |
| **Database Schemas (tested)** | `src/main/resources/db/migration/V*__*.sql` (Flyway) | DataвЂ‘migration and schema integrity. |

*Only the above directories and endpoints are in scope for PhaseвЂЇ4. Any test or artifact outside these boundaries must be deferred to later phases.*

#### 3. Dedicated Sub-Agent Functional Directives
| SubвЂ‘Agent | Specific Tasks (ordered by priority) |
|-----------|----------------------------------------|
| **Manager** | вЂў Finalize test strategy and acceptance criteria. <br> вЂў Approve testвЂ‘environment provisioning (GKE test cluster, test DB snapshots). <br> вЂў Track progress against DoD and raise blockers. |
| **Coder** | вЂў Implement unit tests for `AuthService`, `AttendanceService`, `NotificationService`, and `CenterService` (target в‰ҐвЂЇ90вЂЇ% line coverage). <br> вЂў Write integration tests that spin up Kafka (using `kafka-test-container`) and Postgres (using `testcontainers`) to verify endвЂ‘toвЂ‘end event flow. <br> вЂў Add contract tests (Pact) for `/api/auth/*` and `/api/attendance/*` endpoints. <br> вЂў Integrate test coverage reporting (Jacoco, Istanbul) into CI pipeline. |
| **Tester** | вЂў Design exploratory test cases for QR scanning edgeвЂ‘cases (duplicate scans, expired tokens). <br> вЂў Create UI/E2E test suites (Cypress + Detox) covering multiвЂ‘language locale detection and notification delivery. <br> вЂў Execute security scans (OWASP ZAP) and log findings; remediate highвЂ‘severity issues. <br> вЂў Run performance scripts (k6) to validate throughput under simulated load (target <вЂЇ200вЂЇms p95 latency). <br> вЂў Validate GDPR/CCPA compliance (data export, consent revocation). |
| **Reviewer** | вЂў Conduct peer review of all test code (coding standards, naming, documentation). <br> вЂў Verify test isolation (no sideвЂ‘effects between test runs). <br> вЂў Approve testвЂ‘environment manifests and CI configuration. |
| **DevOps** | вЂў Provision a dedicated GKE test namespace (`membership-hub-test`) with autoscaling enabled. <br> вЂў Deploy testвЂ‘ready Docker images (built with `docker build -t membership-hub:test .`) to the test cluster. <br> вЂў Configure CI pipeline to trigger on PR merge, run unit/integration/UI/security/performance tests, and publish artifacts. <br> вЂў Set up Cloud Logging & Monitoring dashboards for test environment; validate that logs contain request IDs, error traces, and metric exports. |
| **All Agents (collaborative)** | вЂў Ensure test data is sanitized and does not leak PII (GDPR). <br> вЂў Maintain a shared testвЂ‘defect board (Jira) with clear acceptance criteria. <br> вЂў Document any assumptions or limitations in a `TEST-README.md` placed in the root of the repository. |

#### 4. Phase Definition of Done (DoD)
- **Functional Testing** вЂ“ All core API endpoints (`/api/auth/*`, `/api/attendance/*`, `/api/notifications/*`, `/api/centers/*`) pass automated integration tests; QR scanning logic correctly handles duplicates and expiration; notification delivery (SMS/Zalo/app) is verified via mock services.
- **Unit Testing** вЂ“ в‰ҐвЂЇ90вЂЇ% line coverage across all backend services; all unit tests pass in CI; coverage reports are attached to the build artifact.
- **UI/UX Testing** вЂ“ EndвЂ‘toвЂ‘end test suites (Cypress + Detox) execute without failures across supported locales; multiвЂ‘language SEO metaвЂ‘tags are validated; locale detection works as per requirement (user preference в†’ browser в†’ default).
- **Security & Compliance** вЂ“ OWASP ZAP scan reports no highвЂ‘severity vulnerabilities; token handling follows JWT best practices; GDPR/CCPA test scripts confirm data export, consent management, and PII deletion; all security findings are closed or accepted with mitigation.
- **Performance** вЂ“ k6 load tests meet SLA (<вЂЇ200вЂЇms p95 latency, в‰ҐвЂЇ1000вЂЇRPS) under simulated traffic; no memory leaks detected; performance baselines documented.
- **Observability** вЂ“ Cloud Logging captures all request/response logs, error traces, and audit events; Cloud Monitoring dashboards display key metrics (CPU, memory, Kafka lag, error rate); alerts are configured for error thresholds.
- **CI/CD Validation** вЂ“ Automated test pipeline runs successfully on a clean environment; all test artifacts (reports, coverage) are stored and accessible; pipeline fails fast on any test failure or coverage drop.
- **Documentation** вЂ“ `TEST-README.md` includes test execution instructions, environment setup, and known limitations; test results are summarized in a `TEST-REPORT.pdf` attached to the phase deliverable.
- **Approval** вЂ“ Manager, Reviewer, and DevOps sign off that the above criteria are satisfied; no open critical defects remain that would block progression to PhaseвЂЇ5.

*When all DoD items are met, the PhaseвЂЇ4 work is considered complete and the output is frozen.*

# Phase 5 STEPS - Prompt:

Analyze the attached Phase 5 Context Markdown content.


Extract and translate ALL daily steps, checklists, and agent tasks from the entire document.


## CRITICAL TIMELINE BOUNDARY CONSTRAINTS:
#### 1. STRICT PHASE DURATION LIMIT: Each individual Phase MUST be strictly bounded between 1 to 7 days maximum (Absolute Hard Limit: Maximum 7 days per phase). Under no circumstances are you allowed to invent, extrapolate, or generate scheduling logs beyond Day 7.
#### 2. PROGRESSION STOPPING CRITERION: Stop generating immediately once the core technical objectives of the current Phase are satisfied. Do NOT duplicate or loop previous task structures just to inflate the timeline. If the work is complete on Day 1, freeze the output and exit.

## AGENT ATOMICITY CONSTRAINTS (CRITICAL FOR PRODUCTION):
#### 1. ATOMIC AGENT ASSIGNMENT: Every single object inside the 'sub_tasks' array MUST have exactly ONE sub-agent role (string) assigned to the 'agent' field. Dual-agent or multi-agent assignments within a single task object are strictly forbidden.
#### 2. ONE AGENT PER WORK-UNIT: If a workflow step requires collaborative action (e.g., a Coder writes code and a Tester verifies it), you MUST split this action into two distinct, sequential task objects inside the 'sub_tasks' array:
   - Task 1: Assigned to "Coder" with its respective files in 'components'.
   - Task 2: Assigned to "Tester" with its respective verification files in 'components'.
#### 3. COMPONENT SEGREGATION: Ensure that the 'components' array inside each task object ONLY contains the specific files that the assigned agent will touch, create, or modify for that exact step.

## CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS FOR PRODUCTION STABILITY:


#### 1. Target Range Focus: Extract every single scheduled day found in the provided markdown file.
#### 2. Mandatory Data Extraction: You MUST parse and generate a day object node inside the 'days' array for EVERY single day documented in the text.

#### 3. NO ESCAPE HATCH: Do NOT return an empty array for 'days' under any circumstances if there is markdown text present. Even if tasks are not explicitly labeled, parse the paragraph descriptions into technical sub-tasks for that day.
#### 4. STRICT LITERAL FIELD VALUES (MANDATORY):
   - Populate the exact string "{global_context_file}" into the 'global_context_file' field.
   - Populate the exact string "sources/" into the 'source_target_dir' field.
#### 5. Task Details: For every micro task item under a specific day:
   - Provide a sequential task description text into the 'task' field.
   - Provide the assigned role (e.g., 'Coder', 'Tester', 'Reviewer', 'DevOps') into the 'agent', 'subAgent', 'assignee' or 'subAgent' field.
#### 6. Context Fields: For each day object, set 'day' as the integer value of that day, set 'context_file' to '{project_phase_context_file}', and set 'context_section' to 'DAY ' followed by the day number.

You MUST conform strictly to your required JSON Schema layout design structure:
{
  "$defs": {
    "DailyStep": {
      "properties": {
        "day": {
          "description": "Timeline iteration day inside this isolated phase.",
          "title": "Day",
          "type": "integer"
        },
        "context_file": {
          "description": "The phase context Markdown file for closure on this day.",
          "title": "Context File",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "context_section": {
          "description": "The day targeted for closure on this day.",
          "title": "Context Section",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "sub_tasks": {
          "description": "Array of isolated micro-tasks assigned to sub-agents.",
          "items": {
            "$ref": "##/$defs/SubAgentTask"
          },
          "title": "Sub Tasks",
          "type": "array"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "day",
        "context_file",
        "context_section",
        "sub_tasks"
      ],
      "title": "DailyStep",
      "type": "object"
    },
    "SubAgentTask": {
      "properties": {
        "id": {
          "description": "Sub-Task identity of Task that sub-agent role executing.",
          "title": "Id",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "agent": {
          "description": "Target sub-agent role executing the task.",
          "title": "Agent",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "desc": {
          "description": "Literal, low-level technical step assigned to the agent.",
          "title": "Desc",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "components": {
          "description": "Flat array of physical localized file paths or scripts modified or targeted by this single task. Return an empty array [] if no files are involved.",
          "items": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "title": "Components",
          "type": "array"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "id",
        "agent",
        "desc"
      ],
      "title": "SubAgentTask",
      "type": "object"
    }
  },
  "properties": {
    "phase_id": {
      "description": "Target phase tracker index.",
      "title": "Phase Id",
      "type": "integer"
    },
    "phase_name": {
      "description": "Target phase tracker name.",
      "title": "Phase Name",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project_name": {
      "description": "Target project tracker name.",
      "title": "Project Name",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "global_context_file": {
      "description": "Project global context Markdown file for closure.",
      "title": "Global Context File",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "source_target_dir": {
      "description": "Project sources folder path for closure.",
      "title": "Source Target Dir",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "days": {
      "description": "Day-by-day engineering tracking steps.",
      "items": {
        "$ref": "##/$defs/DailyStep"
      },
      "title": "Days",
      "type": "array"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "phase_id",
    "phase_name",
    "project_name",
    "global_context_file",
    "source_target_dir",
    "days"
  ],
  "title": "PhaseStepsPlan",
  "type": "object"
}

--- PHASE 5 CONTEXT MARKDOWN ---
## PHASE 5 CONTEXT BLUEPRINT: test-ai-architecture
#### 1. Phase Operational Scope & Objectives
- **Deploy** the QuarkusвЂ‘KafkaвЂ‘Postgres backend and Next.js mobile/web frontend to **Google Cloud Platform (GCP) вЂ“ GKE** using Docker containers.  
- **Configure** a productionвЂ‘grade Kubernetes environment (namespaces, Ingress, ServiceвЂ‘Mesh if needed) with **horizontal pod autoscaling**, **liveness/readiness probes**, and **network policies** for security.  
- **Implement** comprehensive **monitoring & logging** (Prometheus + Grafana dashboards, Cloud Logging, distributed tracing) to guarantee observability, performance tracking, and rapid incident response.  
- **Secure** the deployment with **IAM**, **TLS/SSL**, and **compliance checks** (GDPR/CCPA) вЂ“ encryption at rest/inвЂ‘transit, audit logs, and secret management (Secret Manager).  
- **Validate** highвЂ‘availability, scalability, and endвЂ‘toвЂ‘end functionality through smoke, load, and security tests.  
- **Document** deployment procedures, runвЂ‘books, and handover artifacts for ongoing operations.  

#### 2. Allowed Technical Scope & Directory Boundaries (Files, paths, and endpoints)
```
project-root/
в”њв”Ђ backend/                     ## Quarkus services
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ src/main/java/...         ## Business logic, Kafka producers/consumers
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ src/main/resources/...    ## Application.yml, schema.sql
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ src/main/docker/         ## Dockerfile (multiвЂ‘stage)
в”‚  в””в”Ђ src/main/kubernetes/     ## K8s deployment/service manifests
в”њв”Ђ frontend/                    ## Next.js (web + mobile)
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ src/                      ## React components, i18n, SEO
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ public/                   ## Static assets
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ Dockerfile                ## MultiвЂ‘stage for Node.js
в”‚  в””в”Ђ src/main/kubernetes/     ## K8s frontend service
в”њв”Ђ docker/                      ## Shared build scripts, dockerвЂ‘compose for local CI
в”њв”Ђ k8s/                         ## Helm charts / raw manifests for GKE
в”‚  в”њв”Ђ charts/
в”‚  в”‚  в”њв”Ђ backend/
в”‚  в”‚  в””в”Ђ frontend/
в”‚  в””в”Ђ monitoring/               ## PrometheusRule, GrafanaDashboard YAMLs
в”њв”Ђ monitoring/                  ## Prometheus config, alerting rules
в”њв”Ђ docs/                        ## Deployment guides, runвЂ‘books
в””в”Ђ ci-cd/                       ## GitHub Actions workflows (build, test, deploy)
```
- **Endpoints** to be exposed (via Ingress):
  - `https://api.membership-hub.example.com/` (Quarkus REST API)
  - `https://app.membership-hub.example.com/` (Next.js web UI)
  - `https://mobile.membership-hub.example.com/` (PWA for mobile)
- **Health / Metrics** endpoints:
  - `/q/health` (Quarkus liveness/readiness)
  - `/metrics` (Prometheus metrics)
  - `/ping` (frontend health check)

#### 3. Dedicated Sub-Agent Functional Directives
###### Manager
- Finalize **deployment acceptance criteria** with product owner and security/compliance teams.  
- Coordinate **goвЂ‘live checklist** and schedule **postвЂ‘deployment review** with stakeholders.  

###### Coder
- Produce **productionвЂ‘ready Dockerfiles** for Quarkus (Java 21, GraalVM optional) and Next.js (Node 20) вЂ“ multiвЂ‘stage, minimal layers.  
- Add **observability hooks**: expose `/metrics`, `/health`, and OpenTelemetry instrumentation for tracing.  
- Commit **K8s manifests** (Deployment, Service, Ingress, HPA, NetworkPolicy) to `src/main/kubernetes/` and `k8s/charts/`.  
- Ensure **configuration** (DB connection, Kafka bootstrap, Firebase/Google/Facebook auth credentials) is injected via **Secret Manager** or environment variables вЂ“ no hardвЂ‘coded secrets.  
- Run **local Docker Compose** smoke test against a temporary GKE sandbox to verify container startup and health endpoints.  

###### Tester
- Design **deployment smoke tests** (curl `/q/health`, `/metrics` on each service).  
- Execute **load tests** (k6 or Locust) against the deployed API to validate HPA behavior and throughput.  
- Perform **security scans** (Trivy for images, OWASP ZAP for runtime) and verify **network policies** block unintended pod communication.  
- Validate **endвЂ‘toвЂ‘end user flows** (login internal/Firebase/Google/Facebook в†’ QR checkвЂ‘in в†’ notification) against the live environment.  
- Capture **monitoring alerts** and confirm they fire correctly for failures.  

###### Reviewer
- Conduct **code review** of all Dockerfiles, K8s manifests, and CI/CD pipeline scripts for adherence to enterprise coding standards and security best practices.  
- Verify **Helm chart versioning**, `values.yaml` templating, and that all secrets are referenced via `SecretRef`.  
- Approve **observability configurations** (PrometheusRule, GrafanaDashboard) for completeness and naming conventions.  

###### DevOps
- Provision **GKE cluster** (or reuse existing) with **node pools** tuned for the workload (e.g., balanced CPU/memory).  
- Apply **Kubernetes configurations**:
  - Namespace `membership-hub-prod`.
  - Deploy via **Helm** (or `kubectl apply`) using charts under `k8s/charts/`.
  - Configure **Ingress** (Google Cloud HTTP(S) LB) with **certвЂ‘manager** for automated TLS.
  - Set up **Horizontal Pod Autoscaler** based on CPU/Memory metrics.
  - Enforce **Network Policies** to isolate backend, frontend, and database pods.
- Install **monitoring stack**:
  - Deploy **Prometheus Operator** and **Grafana** via Helm.
  - Import **preвЂ‘built dashboards** from `k8s/monitoring/` and add custom panels for business metrics (e.g., attendance count, login attempts).
  - Define **Alertmanager** rules for pod restarts, high latency, and database connection errors.
- Integrate **Cloud Logging**: forward container logs to **Stackdriver** via sidecar or GKE logging agent.
- Set up **CI/CD pipeline** (GitHub Actions) that:
  - Runs unit & integration tests.
  - Builds and pushes Docker images to **Artifact Registry**.
  - Triggers **Helm upgrade** on the GKE cluster with rollback capability.
- Perform **postвЂ‘deployment validation**: verify all services are reachable, health checks pass, autoscaling triggers, and monitoring dashboards are populated.
- Document **runвЂ‘books** (restart procedures, scaling actions, log analysis) in `docs/` and share with operations team.  

#### 4. Phase Definition of Done (DoD)
- вњ… All Docker images built, scanned, and pushed to GCP Artifact Registry.  
- вњ… Backend and frontend services deployed in GKE, with **ready** Ingress URLs (`api`, `app`, `mobile`).  
- вњ… **Liveness** and **readiness** probes configured and passing for every pod.  
- вњ… **Horizontal Pod Autoscaler** active and tested under load.  
- вњ… **Network policies** applied and validated (no crossвЂ‘namespace pod exposure).  
- вњ… **TLS/SSL** enabled via certвЂ‘manager; domain certificates installed.  
- вњ… **IAM** and **Secret Manager** policies secured; no plaintext credentials in repo.  
- вњ… **Monitoring** stack operational:
  - Prometheus scrapes metrics from all services.  
  - Grafana dashboards display key business and infrastructure metrics.  
  - Alertmanager rules firing for critical events.  
- вњ… **Logging** flowing to Cloud Logging; structured log format for audit and debugging.  
- вњ… **Security & compliance** scans passed (Trivy, OWASP ZAP, GDPR/CCPA checks).  
- вњ… **Smoke, load, and endвЂ‘toвЂ‘end tests** executed and all passed.  
- вњ… **Documentation** (deployment guides, runвЂ‘books, monitoring dashboards) completed and stored in `docs/`.  
- вњ… **Stakeholder signвЂ‘off** obtained; project manager confirms readiness for production goвЂ‘live.  

*Phase 5 is complete when every item above is verified and signed off.*
------------------------------------------

# Phase 1 STEPS - Prompt:

Analyze the attached Phase 1 Context Markdown content.


Extract and translate ALL daily steps, checklists, and agent tasks from the entire document.


## CRITICAL TIMELINE BOUNDARY CONSTRAINTS:
#### 1. STRICT PHASE DURATION LIMIT: Each individual Phase MUST be strictly bounded between 1 to 7 days maximum (Absolute Hard Limit: Maximum 7 days per phase). Under no circumstances are you allowed to invent, extrapolate, or generate scheduling logs beyond Day 7.
#### 2. PROGRESSION STOPPING CRITERION: Stop generating immediately once the core technical objectives of the current Phase are satisfied. Do NOT duplicate or loop previous task structures just to inflate the timeline. If the work is complete on Day 1, freeze the output and exit.

## AGENT ATOMICITY CONSTRAINTS (CRITICAL FOR PRODUCTION):
#### 1. ATOMIC AGENT ASSIGNMENT: Every single object inside the 'sub_tasks' array MUST have exactly ONE sub-agent role (string) assigned to the 'agent' field. Dual-agent or multi-agent assignments within a single task object are strictly forbidden.
#### 2. ONE AGENT PER WORK-UNIT: If a workflow step requires collaborative action (e.g., a Coder writes code and a Tester verifies it), you MUST split this action into two distinct, sequential task objects inside the 'sub_tasks' array:
   - Task 1: Assigned to "Coder" with its respective files in 'components'.
   - Task 2: Assigned to "Tester" with its respective verification files in 'components'.
#### 3. COMPONENT SEGREGATION: Ensure that the 'components' array inside each task object ONLY contains the specific files that the assigned agent will touch, create, or modify for that exact step.

## CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS FOR PRODUCTION STABILITY:


#### 1. Target Range Focus: Extract every single scheduled day found in the provided markdown file.
#### 2. Mandatory Data Extraction: You MUST parse and generate a day object node inside the 'days' array for EVERY single day documented in the text.

#### 3. NO ESCAPE HATCH: Do NOT return an empty array for 'days' under any circumstances if there is markdown text present. Even if tasks are not explicitly labeled, parse the paragraph descriptions into technical sub-tasks for that day.
#### 4. STRICT LITERAL FIELD VALUES (MANDATORY):
   - Populate the exact string "{global_context_file}" into the 'global_context_file' field.
   - Populate the exact string "sources/" into the 'source_target_dir' field.
#### 5. Task Details: For every micro task item under a specific day:
   - Provide a sequential task description text into the 'task' field.
   - Provide the assigned role (e.g., 'Coder', 'Tester', 'Reviewer', 'DevOps') into the 'agent', 'subAgent', 'assignee' or 'subAgent' field.
#### 6. Context Fields: For each day object, set 'day' as the integer value of that day, set 'context_file' to '{project_phase_context_file}', and set 'context_section' to 'DAY ' followed by the day number.
#### 7. CHRONOLOGICAL TIMELINE RESET MANDATE (ABSOLUTE):
Regardless of the actual day numbers documented in the source Markdown content (e.g., even if the text states "Days 4-7" or "Day 4 to Day 7"), you MUST reset the timeline timeline sequence internally for this JSON output so that it strictly starts from integer 1.
   - The first operational day processed inside this Phase MUST always be gaged as "day": 1.
   - Sequential days must progress logically as 2, 3, 4, etc.
   - Example Transformation: Map "Day 4" to "day": 1, "Day 5" to "day": 2, "Day 6" to "day": 3, and "Day 7" to "day": 4.
   - Adjust the 'context_section' field accordingly to match this localized reset format (e.g., set to "DAY 1", "DAY 2", etc.).

You MUST conform strictly to your required JSON Schema layout design structure:
{
  "$defs": {
    "DailyStep": {
      "properties": {
        "day": {
          "description": "Timeline iteration day inside this isolated phase.",
          "title": "Day",
          "type": "integer"
        },
        "context_file": {
          "description": "The phase context Markdown file for closure on this day.",
          "title": "Context File",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "context_section": {
          "description": "The day targeted for closure on this day.",
          "title": "Context Section",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "sub_tasks": {
          "description": "Array of isolated micro-tasks assigned to sub-agents.",
          "items": {
            "$ref": "##/$defs/SubAgentTask"
          },
          "title": "Sub Tasks",
          "type": "array"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "day",
        "context_file",
        "context_section",
        "sub_tasks"
      ],
      "title": "DailyStep",
      "type": "object"
    },
    "SubAgentTask": {
      "properties": {
        "id": {
          "description": "Sub-Task identity of Task that sub-agent role executing.",
          "title": "Id",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "agent": {
          "description": "Target sub-agent role executing the task.",
          "title": "Agent",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "desc": {
          "description": "Literal, low-level technical step assigned to the agent.",
          "title": "Desc",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "components": {
          "description": "Flat array of physical localized file paths or scripts modified or targeted by this single task. Return an empty array [] if no files are involved.",
          "items": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "title": "Components",
          "type": "array"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "id",
        "agent",
        "desc"
      ],
      "title": "SubAgentTask",
      "type": "object"
    }
  },
  "properties": {
    "phase_id": {
      "description": "Target phase tracker index.",
      "title": "Phase Id",
      "type": "integer"
    },
    "phase_name": {
      "description": "Target phase tracker name.",
      "title": "Phase Name",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project_name": {
      "description": "Target project tracker name.",

      "type": "string"
    },
    "global_context_file": {
      "description": "Project global context Markdown file for closure.",

      "type": "string"
    },
    "source_target_dir": {
      "description": "Project sources folder path for closure.",
      "title": "Source Target Dir",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "days": {
      "description": "Day-by-day engineering tracking steps.",
      "items": {
        "$ref": "##/$defs/DailyStep"
      },
      "title": "Days",
      "type": "array"
    }
  },

    "phase_id",
    "phase_name",
    "project_name",
    "global_context_file",
    "source_target_dir",
    "days"
  ],
  "title": "PhaseStepsPlan",
  "type": "object"
}

--- PHASE 1 CONTEXT MARKDOWN ---
## PHASE 1 CONTEXT BLUEPRINT: test-ai-architecture
#### 1. Phase Operational Scope & Objectives
| Item | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Goal** | Establish a complete, actionable plan for the *membershipвЂ‘hub* platform covering web & mobile frontвЂ‘ends, QuarkusвЂ‘based microвЂ‘services, Kafka event streaming, PostgreSQL persistence, Docker containerisation, and GCPвЂ‘GKE deployment. |
| **Key Activities (DaysвЂЇ1вЂ‘3)** | вЂў Conduct stakeholder interviews & workshops to capture functional & nonвЂ‘functional requirements.<br>вЂў Document user stories, acceptance criteria, and edge cases (including QRвЂ‘based attendance, multiвЂ‘auth, multiвЂ‘language, SEO, notifications).<br>вЂў Define technical architecture (service boundaries, data flow, authentication flow, event schema).<br>вЂў Produce a detailed project plan with 5вЂ‘phase timeline, sprintвЂ‘level tasks, resource allocation, and risk register.<br>вЂў Set up the initial repository structure, CI/CD skeleton, and environment manifests. |
| **Deliverables** | вЂў Requirements Specification Document (RSD).<br>вЂў User Story Map & Acceptance Criteria.<br>вЂў Technical Architecture Diagram (including service contracts, Kafka topics, auth providers, GCP resources).<br>вЂў Project Plan & Gantt (PhaseвЂ‘level, with DayвЂ‘1вЂ‘toвЂ‘DayвЂ‘3 milestones).<br>вЂў Repository with `README`, `CONTRIBUTING`, `ISSUE_TEMPLATE`, and placeholder `Dockerfile`s / `k8s` manifests. |
| **Constraints** | вЂў Strict 7вЂ‘day max per phase вЂ“ PhaseвЂЇ1 must be completed within DaysвЂЇ1вЂ‘3.<br>вЂў No code implementation beyond scaffolding; focus on documentation & planning artifacts.<br>вЂў All decisions must align with Global Guardrails (Security, Scalability, Compliance, Quality, Monitoring). |

#### 2. Allowed Technical Scope & Directory Boundaries (Files, paths, and endpoints)
```
membership-hub/
в”њв”Ђ docs/                     ## Architecture & design docs
в”‚   в”њв”Ђ architecture.md
в”‚   в””в”Ђ requirements.md
в”њв”Ђ src/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђ backend/              ## Quarkus microвЂ‘service skeleton
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђ src/main/java/com/hub/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђ src/main/resources/application.yml
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђ pom.xml
в”‚   в”њв”Ђ frontend/             ## Next.js monorepo (web + mobile web view)
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђ web/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђ mobile/
в”‚   в””в”Ђ shared/               ## Common models, enums, constants
в”њв”Ђ kafka/
в”‚   в””в”Ђ topics/               ## Event schema definitions (Avro/Protobuf)
в”њв”Ђ db/
в”‚   в””в”Ђ migrations/           ## Flyway/Liquibase scripts for Postgres
в”њв”Ђ docker/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђ Dockerfile.backend
в”‚   в””в”Ђ Dockerfile.frontend
в”њв”Ђ k8s/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђ backend/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђ deployment.yaml
в”‚   в””в”Ђ frontend/
в”‚       в””в”Ђ deployment.yaml
в”њв”Ђ .github/
в”‚   в””в”Ђ workflows/
в”‚       в””в”Ђ ci.yml            ## CI pipeline (build, lint, test)
в”њв”Ђ .gitlab-ci.yml (if used)  ## CI/CD definitions
в”њв”Ђ README.md
в”њв”Ђ CONTRIBUTING.md
в””в”Ђ LICENSE
```
**Allowed Endpoints (to be defined in PhaseвЂЇ1)**  
- `POST /api/auth/internal` вЂ“ email/password signвЂ‘in.  
- `POST /api/auth/firebase`, `POST /api/auth/google`, `POST /api/auth/facebook` вЂ“ external OAuth flows.  
- `GET/POST /api/students/{id}/attendance` вЂ“ QR checkвЂ‘in (idempotent per day).  
- `GET /api/students/{id}/card` вЂ“ remaining validity days.  
- `POST /api/notifications` вЂ“ push/Zalo message dispatch.  

All other implementation details (service logic, UI components, Kafka producers/consumers) are **out of scope** for PhaseвЂЇ1.

#### 3. Dedicated Sub-Agent Functional Directives (Specific tasks for Coder, Tester, Reviewer, DevOps, etc.)

| SubвЂ‘Agent | PhaseвЂЇ1 Specific Tasks |
|-----------|------------------------|
| **Manager** | вЂў Schedule and conduct 3 stakeholder workshops (client, product owner, security/compliance).<br>вЂў Capture вЂњmustвЂ‘haveвЂќ, вЂњshouldвЂ‘haveвЂќ, вЂњcouldвЂ‘haveвЂќ items; map to epics.<br>вЂў Draft the Requirements Specification Document (RSD) with acceptance criteria.<br>вЂў Produce the highвЂ‘level technical architecture diagram (service boundaries, data flow, auth providers, GCP resources).<br>вЂў Create the PhaseвЂЇ1 project plan (DayвЂ‘1вЂ‘toвЂ‘DayвЂ‘3 tasks, dependencies, owners). |
| **Coder** | вЂў Initialise the Git repository with the directory layout shown above.<br>вЂў Create placeholder `pom.xml` (Quarkus) and `package.json`/`next.config.js` (Next.js) to satisfy build pipelines.<br>вЂў Add basic `Dockerfile`s (backend & frontend) with multiвЂ‘stage builds.<br>вЂў Draft Kafka topic definitions (Avro schemas) in `kafka/topics/`.<br>вЂў Add Flyway migration skeleton for core tables (`students`, `attendance`, `cards`, `notifications`). |
| **Tester** | вЂў Define the Test Strategy document (unit, integration, contract, security, performance).<br>вЂў Draft testвЂ‘case templates for authentication flows, attendance idempotency, notification delivery, and multiвЂ‘language SEO.<br>вЂў Create a lightweight testвЂ‘plan checklist aligned with the acceptance criteria.<br>вЂў Set up a shared testвЂ‘environment manifest (DockerвЂ‘Compose for local dev). |
| **Reviewer** | вЂў Publish CodeвЂ‘Review Guidelines (Java/Quarkus, TypeScript/Next.js, Docker, YAML).<br>вЂў Define security checklist (JWT handling, OAuth scopes, data encryption, GDPR/CCPA dataвЂ‘handling).<br>вЂў Review all PhaseвЂЇ1 artifacts (RSD, architecture diagram, code scaffolding) for compliance with guidelines. |
| **DevOps** | вЂў Create the CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) that runs `mvn clean package` and `npm run build`.<br>вЂў Draft Kubernetes manifests (`k8s/backend/deployment.yaml`, `k8s/frontend/deployment.yaml`) with resource limits & autoscaling hints.<br>вЂў Add a `monitoring/` folder with Prometheus/Grafana dashboards skeleton and logging configuration (ELK/Stackdriver).<br>вЂў Document localвЂ‘dev setup (DockerвЂ‘Compose) and GCPвЂ‘GKE deployment steps. |

#### 4. Phase Definition of Done (DoD)
- **[RSD Completed]** All functional & nonвЂ‘functional requirements captured, with acceptance criteria and priority levels.
- **[Architecture Documented]** Technical architecture diagram and service contracts approved by stakeholders.
- **[Project Plan Finalised]** PhaseвЂЇ1 Gantt, task breakdown, owner assignments, and risk register signed off.
- **[Repository Ready]** Git repo initialised with the directory structure, `README`, `CONTRIBUTING`, `ISSUE_TEMPLATE`, and placeholder CI/CD files.
- **[Scaffolding Created]** Maven/Quarkus skeleton, Next.js monorepo skeleton, Dockerfiles, Kafka schema placeholders, Flyway migrations.
- **[Test Strategy Defined]** TestвЂ‘plan, testвЂ‘case templates, and localвЂ‘dev environment manifest ready.
- **[Review Guidelines Issued]** CodeвЂ‘review checklist, security checklist, and documentation of standards.
- **[CI/CD Skeleton Built]** Automated build pipeline (Maven + Next.js) and Kubernetes deployment manifests prepared.
- **[Monitoring Blueprint]** Basic monitoring and logging configuration (metrics, alerts, logs) documented.
- **[SignвЂ‘off]** All subвЂ‘agents have reviewed and signed off the PhaseвЂЇ1 deliverables; no outstanding tasks remain before moving to PhaseвЂЇ2.

## PHASE 1 CONTEXT BLUEPRINT: test-ai-architecture
#### 1. Phase Operational Scope & Objectives
| Item | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Goal** | Establish a complete, actionable plan for the *membershipвЂ‘hub* platform covering web & mobile frontвЂ‘ends, QuarkusвЂ‘based microвЂ‘services, Kafka event streaming, PostgreSQL persistence, Docker containerisation, and GCPвЂ‘GKE deployment. |
| **Key Activities (DaysвЂЇ1вЂ‘3)** | вЂў Conduct stakeholder interviews & workshops to capture functional & nonвЂ‘functional requirements.<br>вЂў Document user stories, acceptance criteria, and edge cases (including QRвЂ‘based attendance, multiвЂ‘auth, multiвЂ‘language, SEO, notifications).<br>вЂў Define technical architecture (service boundaries, data flow, authentication flow, event schema).<br>вЂў Produce a detailed project plan with 5вЂ‘phase timeline, sprintвЂ‘level tasks, resource allocation, and risk register.<br>вЂў Set up the initial repository structure, CI/CD skeleton, and environment manifests. |
| **Deliverables** | вЂў Requirements Specification Document (RSD).<br>вЂў User Story Map & Acceptance Criteria.<br>вЂў Technical Architecture Diagram (including service contracts, Kafka topics, auth providers, GCP resources).<br>вЂў Project Plan & Gantt (PhaseвЂ‘level, with DayвЂ‘1вЂ‘toвЂ‘DayвЂ‘3 milestones).<br>вЂў Repository with `README`, `CONTRIBUTING`, `ISSUE_TEMPLATE`, and placeholder `Dockerfile`s / `k8s` manifests. |
| **Constraints** | вЂў Strict 7вЂ‘day max per phase вЂ“ PhaseвЂЇ1 must be completed within DaysвЂЇ1вЂ‘3.<br>вЂў No code implementation beyond scaffolding; focus on documentation & planning artifacts.<br>вЂў All decisions must align with Global Guardrails (Security, Scalability, Compliance, Quality, Monitoring). |

#### 2. Allowed Technical Scope & Directory Boundaries (Files, paths, and endpoints)
```
membership-hub/
в”њв”Ђ docs/                     ## Architecture & design docs
в”‚   в”њв”Ђ architecture.md
в”‚   в””в”Ђ requirements.md
в”њв”Ђ src/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђ backend/              ## Quarkus microвЂ‘service skeleton
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђ src/main/java/com/hub/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђ src/main/resources/application.yml
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђ pom.xml
в”‚   в”њв”Ђ frontend/             ## Next.js monorepo (web + mobile web view)
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђ web/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђ mobile/
в”‚   в””в”Ђ shared/               ## Common models, enums, constants
в”њв”Ђ kafka/
в”‚   в””в”Ђ topics/               ## Event schema definitions (Avro/Protobuf)
в”њв”Ђ db/
в”‚   в””в”Ђ migrations/           ## Flyway/Liquibase scripts for Postgres
в”њв”Ђ docker/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђ Dockerfile.backend
в”‚   в””в”Ђ Dockerfile.frontend
в”њв”Ђ k8s/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђ backend/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђ deployment.yaml
в”‚   в””в”Ђ frontend/
в”‚       в””в”Ђ deployment.yaml
в”њв”Ђ .github/
в”‚   в””в”Ђ workflows/
в”‚       в””в”Ђ ci.yml            ## CI pipeline (build, lint, test)
в”њв”Ђ .gitlab-ci.yml (if used)  ## CI/CD definitions
в”њв”Ђ README.md
в”њв”Ђ CONTRIBUTING.md
в””в”Ђ LICENSE
```
**Allowed Endpoints (to be defined in PhaseвЂЇ1)**  
- `POST /api/auth/internal` вЂ“ email/password signвЂ‘in.  
- `POST /api/auth/firebase`, `POST /api/auth/google`, `POST /api/auth/facebook` вЂ“ external OAuth flows.  
- `GET/POST /api/students/{id}/attendance` вЂ“ QR checkвЂ‘in (idempotent per day).  
- `GET /api/students/{id}/card` вЂ“ remaining validity days.  
- `POST /api/notifications` вЂ“ push/Zalo message dispatch.  

All other implementation details (service logic, UI components, Kafka producers/consumers) are **out of scope** for PhaseвЂЇ1.

#### 3. Dedicated Sub-Agent Functional Directives (Specific tasks for Coder, Tester, Reviewer, DevOps, etc.)

| SubвЂ‘Agent | PhaseвЂЇ1 Specific Tasks |
|-----------|------------------------|
| **Manager** | вЂў Schedule and conduct 3 stakeholder workshops (client, product owner, security/compliance).<br>вЂў Capture вЂњmustвЂ‘haveвЂќ, вЂњshouldвЂ‘haveвЂќ, вЂњcouldвЂ‘haveвЂќ items; map to epics.<br>вЂў Draft the Requirements Specification Document (RSD) with acceptance criteria.<br>вЂў Produce the highвЂ‘level technical architecture diagram (service boundaries, data flow, auth providers, GCP resources).<br>вЂў Create the PhaseвЂЇ1 project plan (DayвЂ‘1вЂ‘toвЂ‘DayвЂ‘3 tasks, dependencies, owners). |
| **Coder** | вЂў Initialise the Git repository with the directory layout shown above.<br>вЂў Create placeholder `pom.xml` (Quarkus) and `package.json`/`next.config.js` (Next.js) to satisfy build pipelines.<br>вЂў Add basic `Dockerfile`s (backend & frontend) with multiвЂ‘stage builds.<br>вЂў Draft Kafka topic definitions (Avro schemas) in `kafka/topics/`.<br>вЂў Add Flyway migration skeleton for core tables (`students`, `attendance`, `cards`, `notifications`). |
| **Tester** | вЂў Define the Test Strategy document (unit, integration, contract, security, performance).<br>вЂў Draft testвЂ‘case templates for authentication flows, attendance idempotency, notification delivery, and multiвЂ‘language SEO.<br>вЂў Create a lightweight testвЂ‘plan checklist aligned with the acceptance criteria.<br>вЂў Set up a shared testвЂ‘environment manifest (DockerвЂ‘Compose for local dev). |
| **Reviewer** | вЂў Publish CodeвЂ‘Review Guidelines (Java/Quarkus, TypeScript/Next.js, Docker, YAML).<br>вЂў Define security checklist (JWT handling, OAuth scopes, data encryption, GDPR/CCPA dataвЂ‘handling).<br>вЂў Review all PhaseвЂЇ1 artifacts (RSD, architecture diagram, code scaffolding) for compliance with guidelines. |
| **DevOps** | вЂў Create the CI workflow (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) that runs `mvn clean package` and `npm run build`.<br>вЂў Draft Kubernetes manifests (`k8s/backend/deployment.yaml`, `k8s/frontend/deployment.yaml`) with resource limits & autoscaling hints.<br>вЂў Add a `monitoring/` folder with Prometheus/Grafana dashboards skeleton and logging configuration (ELK/Stackdriver).<br>вЂў Document localвЂ‘dev setup (DockerвЂ‘Compose) and GCPвЂ‘GKE deployment steps. |

#### 4. Phase Definition of Done (DoD)
- **[RSD Completed]** All functional & nonвЂ‘functional requirements captured, with acceptance criteria and priority levels.
- **[Architecture Documented]** Technical architecture diagram and service contracts approved by stakeholders.
- **[Project Plan Finalised]** PhaseвЂЇ1 Gantt, task breakdown, owner assignments, and risk register signed off.
- **[Repository Ready]** Git repo initialised with the directory structure, `README`, `CONTRIBUTING`, `ISSUE_TEMPLATE`, and placeholder CI/CD files.
- **[Scaffolding Created]** Maven/Quarkus skeleton, Next.js monorepo skeleton, Dockerfiles, Kafka schema placeholders, Flyway migrations.
- **[Test Strategy Defined]** TestвЂ‘plan, testвЂ‘case templates, and localвЂ‘dev environment manifest ready.
- **[Review Guidelines Issued]** CodeвЂ‘review checklist, security checklist, and documentation of standards.
- **[CI/CD Skeleton Built]** Automated build pipeline (Maven + Next.js) and Kubernetes deployment manifests prepared.
- **[Monitoring Blueprint]** Basic monitoring and logging configuration (metrics, alerts, logs) documented.
- **[SignвЂ‘off]** All subвЂ‘agents have reviewed and signed off the PhaseвЂЇ1 deliverables; no outstanding tasks remain before moving to PhaseвЂЇ2.

# Phase 1 STEPS - Prompt:

Analyze the attached Phase 1 Context Markdown content.


Extract and translate ALL daily steps, checklists, and agent tasks from the entire document.


## CRITICAL TIMELINE BOUNDARY CONSTRAINTS:
#### 1. STRICT PHASE DURATION LIMIT: Each individual Phase MUST be strictly bounded between 1 to 7 days maximum (Absolute Hard Limit: Maximum 7 days per phase). Under no circumstances are you allowed to invent, extrapolate, or generate scheduling logs beyond Day 7.
#### 2. PROGRESSION STOPPING CRITERION: Stop generating immediately once the core technical objectives of the current Phase are satisfied. Do NOT duplicate or loop previous task structures just to inflate the timeline. If the work is complete on Day 1, freeze the output and exit.

## AGENT ATOMICITY CONSTRAINTS (CRITICAL FOR PRODUCTION):
#### 1. ATOMIC AGENT ASSIGNMENT: Every single object inside the 'sub_tasks' array MUST have exactly ONE sub-agent role (string) assigned to the 'agent' field. Dual-agent or multi-agent assignments within a single task object are strictly forbidden.
#### 2. ONE AGENT PER WORK-UNIT: If a workflow step requires collaborative action (e.g., a Coder writes code and a Tester verifies it), you MUST split this action into two distinct, sequential task objects inside the 'sub_tasks' array:
   - Task 1: Assigned to "Coder" with its respective files in 'components'.
   - Task 2: Assigned to "Tester" with its respective verification files in 'components'.
#### 3. COMPONENT SEGREGATION: Ensure that the 'components' array inside each task object ONLY contains the specific files that the assigned agent will touch, create, or modify for that exact step.

## CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS FOR PRODUCTION STABILITY:


#### 1. Target Range Focus: Extract every single scheduled day found in the provided markdown file.
#### 2. Mandatory Data Extraction: You MUST parse and generate a day object node inside the 'days' array for EVERY single day documented in the text.

#### 3. NO ESCAPE HATCH: Do NOT return an empty array for 'days' under any circumstances if there is markdown text present. Even if tasks are not explicitly labeled, parse the paragraph descriptions into technical sub-tasks for that day.
#### 4. STRICT LITERAL FIELD VALUES (MANDATORY):
   - Populate the exact string "{global_context_file}" into the 'global_context_file' field.
   - Populate the exact string "sources/" into the 'source_target_dir' field.
#### 5. Task Details: For every micro task item under a specific day:
   - Provide a sequential task description text into the 'task' field.
   - Provide the assigned role (e.g., 'Coder', 'Tester', 'Reviewer', 'DevOps') into the 'agent', 'subAgent', 'assignee' or 'subAgent' field.
#### 6. Context Fields: For each day object, set 'day' as the integer value of that day, set 'context_file' to '{project_phase_context_file}', and set 'context_section' to 'DAY ' followed by the day number.
#### 7. CHRONOLOGICAL TIMELINE RESET MANDATE (ABSOLUTE):
Regardless of the actual day numbers documented in the source Markdown content (e.g., even if the text states "Days 4-7" or "Day 4 to Day 7"), you MUST reset the timeline timeline sequence internally for this JSON output so that it strictly starts from integer 1.
   - The first operational day processed inside this Phase MUST always be gaged as "day": 1.
   - Sequential days must progress logically as 2, 3, 4, etc.
   - Example Transformation: Map "Day 4" to "day": 1, "Day 5" to "day": 2, "Day 6" to "day": 3, and "Day 7" to "day": 4.
   - Adjust the 'context_section' field accordingly to match this localized reset format (e.g., set to "DAY 1", "DAY 2", etc.).

You MUST conform strictly to your required JSON Schema layout design structure:
{
  "$defs": {
    "DailyStep": {
      "properties": {
        "day": {
          "description": "Timeline iteration day inside this isolated phase.",
          "title": "Day",
          "type": "integer"
        },
        "context_file": {
          "description": "The phase context Markdown file for closure on this day.",
          "title": "Context File",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "context_section": {
          "description": "The day targeted for closure on this day.",
          "title": "Context Section",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "sub_tasks": {
          "description": "Array of isolated micro-tasks assigned to sub-agents.",
          "items": {
            "$ref": "##/$defs/SubAgentTask"
          },
          "title": "Sub Tasks",
          "type": "array"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "day",
        "context_file",
        "context_section",
        "sub_tasks"
      ],
      "title": "DailyStep",
      "type": "object"
    },
    "SubAgentTask": {
      "properties": {
        "id": {
          "description": "Sub-Task identity of Task that sub-agent role executing.",
          "title": "Id",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "agent": {
          "description": "Target sub-agent role executing the task.",
          "title": "Agent",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "desc": {
          "description": "Literal, low-level technical step assigned to the agent.",
          "title": "Desc",
          "type": "string"
        },
        "components": {
          "description": "Flat array of physical localized file paths or scripts modified or targeted by this single task. Return an empty array [] if no files are involved.",
          "items": {
            "type": "string"
          },
          "title": "Components",
          "type": "array"
        }
      },
      "required": [
        "id",
        "agent",
        "desc"
      ],
      "title": "SubAgentTask",
      "type": "object"
    }
  },
  "properties": {
    "phase_id": {
      "description": "Target phase tracker index.",
      "title": "Phase Id",
      "type": "integer"
    },
    "phase_name": {
      "description": "Target phase tracker name.",
      "title": "Phase Name",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project_name": {
      "description": "Target project tracker name.",
      "title": "Project Name",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "global_context_file": {
      "description": "Project global context Markdown file for closure.",
      "title": "Global Context File",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "source_target_dir": {
      "description": "Project sources folder path for closure.",
      "title": "Source Target Dir",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "days": {
      "description": "Day-by-day engineering tracking steps.",
      "items": {
        "$ref": "##/$defs/DailyStep"
      },
      "title": "Days",
      "type": "array"
    }
  },

  ],

## CRITICAL TIMELINE BOUNDARY CONSTRAINTS:
#### 1. STRICT PHASE DURATION LIMIT: Each individual Phase MUST be strictly bounded between 1 to 7 days maximum (Absolute Hard Limit: Maximum 7 days per phase). Under no circumstances are you allowed to invent, extrapolate, or generate scheduling logs beyond Day 7.
#### 2. PROGRESSION STOPPING CRITERION: Stop generating immediately once the core technical objectives of the current Phase are satisfied. Do NOT duplicate or loop previous task structures just to inflate the timeline. If the work is complete on Day 1, freeze the output and exit.

## AGENT ATOMICITY CONSTRAINTS (CRITICAL FOR PRODUCTION):
#### 1. ATOMIC AGENT ASSIGNMENT: Every single object inside the 'sub_tasks' array MUST have exactly ONE sub-agent role (string) assigned to the 'agent' field. Dual-agent or multi-agent assignments within a single task object are strictly forbidden.
#### 2. ONE AGENT PER WORK-UNIT: If a workflow step requires collaborative action (e.g., a Coder writes code and a Tester verifies it), you MUST split this action into two distinct, sequential task objects inside the 'sub_tasks' array:
   - Task 1: Assigned to "Coder" with its respective files in 'components'.
   - Task 2: Assigned to "Tester" with its respective verification files in 'components'.
#### 3. COMPONENT SEGREGATION: Ensure that the 'components' array inside each task object ONLY contains the specific files that the assigned agent will touch, create, or modify for that exact step.

## CRITICAL INSTRUCTIONS FOR PRODUCTION STABILITY:


#### 1. Target Range Focus: Extract every single scheduled day found in the provided markdown file.
#### 2. Mandatory Data Extraction: You MUST parse and generate a day object node inside the 'days' array for EVERY single day documented in the text.

#### 3. NO ESCAPE HATCH: Do NOT return an empty array for 'days' under any circumstances if there is markdown text present. Even if tasks are not explicitly labeled, parse the paragraph descriptions into technical sub-tasks for that day.
#### 4. STRICT LITERAL FIELD VALUES (MANDATORY):
   - Populate the exact string "{global_context_file}" into the 'global_context_file' field.
   - Populate the exact string "sources/" into the 'source_target_dir' field.
#### 5. Task Details: For every micro task item under a specific day:
   - Provide a sequential task description text into the 'task' field.
   - Provide the assigned role (e.g., 'Coder', 'Tester', 'Reviewer', 'DevOps') into the 'agent', 'subAgent', 'assignee' or 'subAgent' field.
#### 6. Context Fields: For each day object, set 'day' as the integer value of that day, set 'context_file' to '{project_phase_context_file}', and set 'context_section' to 'DAY ' followed by the day number.
#### 7. CHRONOLOGICAL TIMELINE RESET MANDATE (ABSOLUTE):
Regardless of the actual day numbers documented in the source Markdown content (e.g., even if the text states "Days 4-7" or "Day 4 to Day 7"), you MUST reset the timeline timeline sequence internally for this JSON output so that it strictly starts from integer 1.
   - The first operational day processed inside this Phase MUST always be gaged as "day": 1.
   - Sequential days must progress logically as 2, 3, 4, etc.
   - Example Transformation: Map "Day 4" to "day": 1, "Day 5" to "day": 2, "Day 6" to "day": 3, and "Day 7" to "day": 4.
   - Adjust the 'context_section' field accordingly to match this localized reset format (e.g., set to "DAY 1", "DAY 2", etc.).

You MUST conform strictly to your required JSON Schema layout design structure:
